proofs.proof_tex:
By definition, W_n^lb(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en) is obtained from W_n(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en) by adding the extra member property ass:regular-cell-mass and keeping the other defining conditions the same. Hence W_n^lb(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en) is a subclass of W_n(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en).

Fix any estimator hat_tau based on O_1, ..., O_n. Since a supremum over a larger class dominates the supremum over a subclass,

sup_{P in W_n(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en)} E_P |hat_tau - tau(P)| >= sup_{P in W_n^lb(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en)} E_P |hat_tau - tau(P)|.

Taking the infimum over all estimators preserves the inequality, so

inf_{hat_tau} sup_{P in W_n(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en)} E_P |hat_tau - tau(P)| >= inf_{hat_tau} sup_{P in W_n^lb(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en)} E_P |hat_tau - tau(P)|.

Now apply conj:tail-lower-bound, which asserts that the right-hand side is at least c R_n^* for some universal constant c > 0. Therefore

inf_{hat_tau} sup_{P in W_n(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en)} E_P |hat_tau - tau(P)| >= c R_n^*.

This proves the lower-bound half of the proposed frontier.

added_lemmas.statement: There exists a universal constant c > 0 such that inf_{hat_tau} sup_{P in W_n(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en)} E_P |hat_tau - tau(P)| >= c R_n^*.
added_lemmas.proof_tex:
By definition, W_n^lb(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en) is obtained from W_n(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en) by adding the extra member property ass:regular-cell-mass and keeping the other defining conditions the same. Hence W_n^lb(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en) is a subclass of W_n(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en).

sup_{P in W_n(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en)} E_P |hat_tau - tau(P)| >= sup_{P in W_n^lb(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en)} E_P |hat_tau - tau(P)|.

This proves the lower-bound half of the proposed frontier.

proposed_statement_changes.current: The minimax absolute risk for tau over W_n(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en) is equivalent, up to universal constants, to R_n^* = inf_{lambda in Lambda_n} R_n(lambda).
proposed_statement_changes.proposed: There exists a universal constant c > 0 such that inf_{hat_tau} sup_{P in W_n(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en)} E_P |hat_tau - tau(P)| >= c R_n^*. Moreover, if the upper theorem is strengthened to a uniform absolute-risk bound, namely if there exist a universal constant C > 0 and a deterministic sequence lambda_n in Lambda_n with R_n(lambda_n) <= 2 R_n^* such that sup_{P in W_n(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en)} E_P |hat_tau_{lambda_n} - tau(P)| <= C U_n(lambda_n), then the minimax absolute risk over W_n(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en) is equivalent, up to universal constants, to R_n^*.
proposed_statement_changes.reason: The lower-bound direction is provable because conj:tail-lower-bound applies on the subclass W_n^lb(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en), and that subclass is contained in W_n(kappa, r_0n, r_1n, r_en). The upper direction is not derivable from the declared dependencies as stated. thm:clipped-upper gives only the pointwise stochastic-order conclusion |hat_tau_lambda - tau| = O_P(U_n(lambda)); it does not give a uniform-over-P bound on E_P |hat_tau_lambda - tau|, which is the quantity entering minimax absolute risk. prop:tail-order-reduction compares U_n(lambda) and R_n(lambda), but it does not upgrade pointwise O_P control to a uniform L1 risk bound. So the unconditional equivalence claim is stronger than what the frozen upper theorem currently supplies.
